text:
Electronic journal *Economic Sociology* was launched in 2000 by Vadim Radaev (now Editor-in-Chief) and since 2007 is officially co-founded by National Research University Higher School of Economics. It has been in continuous publication for over 20 years.

The journal is a professional academic publication which fulfils the requirements of international research level in the context of the Russian economy. It is addressed to Russian and foreign researchers who have made significant contributions to the field of economic sociology, as well as to young scientists publishing the results of their actual research projects. The journal enables young scientists to learn who and what is published in the world, and thus, in addition to publications, it acts as a link between young researchers and the world community.

Economic Sociology gives priority to the following thematic areas: - theories and approaches - sociological analysis of markets and organizations - strategies and behavior of individuals and households in different economic circumstances - the informal economy from different perspectives. Of particular interest are new approaches deriving from the interdisciplinary relationship with the new institutional economics, anthropology, economic psychology and other disciplines from which the economic sociologist can enrich his knowledge and methods.

Econometrics is published bimonthly (five issues per year). From 1 January 2023, all articles published in Econometrics will be Open Access and permanently available on this website free of charge. All submitted manuscripts will undergo rigorous double-blind peer-review process.
